# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when .beads/ detected

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

○ long-running-harness-isy [EPIC] · Polish: Audio engine robustness and error resilience   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Nathan Delacrétaz · Type: epic
Created: 2026-03-29 · Updated: 2026-03-29

DESCRIPTION
Harden the audio engine against crashes, race conditions, and silent failures. Focus on system boundaries and state machine integrity.

## Findings (from audio engine audit)

### Critical
- voice-allocator.ts: Force-allocation to voice 0 when all voices busy (line ~185). If findIdleVoice() and findOldestActive() both return -1, unconditionally steals voice 0 without proper steal sequence. Cuts off active note without crossfade.
- transport-clock.ts: seek() during playback updates playStartContextTime and playStartCursorSeconds non-atomically. Audio thread may read partial update (stale cursor + new start time).
- transport-clock.ts: Loop wrap modulo can divide by zero if loop.end === loop.start due to race condition at boundary.

### High
- look-ahead-scheduler.ts: Callbacks (onTick, onAdvance) invoked without try-catch. Uncaught exception in callback kills the scheduler setInterval entirely -- silent death of all audio scheduling.
- synth-processor.ts: port.onmessage handler assumes e.data is always valid SynthVoiceCommand. Malformed message crashes audio processor.
- mixer-engine.ts: faderTaper() return value not validated before assignment to AudioParam. NaN propagates silently through audio graph.
- undo-manager.ts: If cmd.undo() throws, command is popped from stack but exception propagates. Undo/redo stacks become permanently desynchronized.
- media-pool-manager.ts: If storage.putBlob() succeeds but storage.putMeta() fails, rollback of blob uses silent catch -- orphaned blob in IndexedDB.

### Medium
- engine-context.ts: No try-catch around new AudioContext() constructor. Browsers without Web Audio crash immediately.
- session-schema.ts: Version enforced as z.literal() with no migration path. Session from v2 is permanently unloadable.
- session-recovery.ts: If both full and partial parse fail, returns empty defaults with no user warning about data loss.
- store.ts: moveClip() doesnt validate toTrackId exists. Clip silently moved to non-existent track.
- store.ts: splitClip() returns undefined silently when atTime is outside clip bounds -- caller cant distinguish invalid split from no-op.
- create-effect.ts: disconnect() called on nodes that may never have been connected. Web Audio throws NotSupportedError.

### Low  
- undo-manager.ts: undoStack.shift() is O(n) in a loop, making push O(n^2) when stack is near limit.
- voice-allocator.ts: No timeout for voice stuck in stealing state. Voice pool can be exhausted after repeated steals.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Voice allocator: proper steal with crossfade when forced allocation needed
- Transport seek: atomic update pattern (write both values, then flip flag)
- Loop wrap: guard against zero-length loop region
- Scheduler: try-catch around all callbacks with error logging
- Synth processor: validate message shape before processing
- Mixer: validate faderTaper return value
- Undo manager: restore command to stack if execution throws
- Engine context: graceful error if AudioContext unavailable
- Session recovery: warn user when falling back to empty defaults

## Files
- src/audio/synth/dsp/voice-allocator.ts
- src/audio/transport-clock.ts
- src/audio/look-ahead-scheduler.ts
- src/audio/synth/synth-processor.ts
- src/audio/mixer/mixer-engine.ts
- src/state/undo/shared-undo-manager.ts
- src/audio/media-pool/media-pool-manager.ts
- src/audio/engine-context.ts
- src/state/session/session-recovery.ts
- src/audio/effects/create-effect.ts
- src/state/store.ts

DEPENDS ON
  → ✓ long-running-harness-0yz: Polish: QA verification via browser automation ● P1

Perfect! I've retrieved all six files. Here are their complete contents:

## 1. src/audio/synth/dsp/voice-allocator.ts
Lines 1-272. A 16-voice fixed pool allocator with oldest-steal and 5ms crossfade. Key features:
- Fixed MAX_VOICES = 16 pool (never grows)
- Oldest-steal algorithm when all voices busy
- Crossfade gain applied during voice stealing (STEAL_CROSSFADE_S = 0.005s)
- Legato mode support with pitch glide
- Zero-allocation in allocate/release operations
- Voice states: idle, active, releasing, stealing

## 2. src/audio/transport-clock.ts
Lines 1-192. Transport state machine writing to SharedArrayBuffer for lock-free thread communication. Key features:
- TransportState: stopped, playing, paused
- Cursor tracking with loop wrap detection
- Loop region support with enabled flag
- BPM management (clamped 20-999)
- SAB views for audio-thread communication (cursor, state, BPM)
- getCursorSeconds() computes position without side effects
- updateCursor() advances playback and writes to SAB

## 3. src/audio/look-ahead-scheduler.ts
Lines 1-134. Chris Wilson look-ahead scheduler pattern. Key features:
- setInterval-based (25ms default) scheduling with 100ms look-ahead window
- Syncs beat phase to clock position on seek
- Calls onTick() for beat-level events and onAdvance() for window-level events
- Respects loop boundaries when constraining scheduling window
- Main thread execution (lightweight, non-blocking)

## 4. src/audio/synth/synth-processor.ts
Lines 1-509. AudioWorklet processor with 16 voices, each voice has:
- 2 PolyBLEP oscillators
- Biquad filter (LP/HP/BP)
- ADSR amplitude envelope
- ADSR filter envelope
- Shared LFO1 and LFO2 modulation
- Zero-allocation process() implementation
- Oldest-steal with 5ms crossfade
- 2ms minimum release time
- Legato mode with pitch glide
- Control-rate filter coefficient recomputation at s=0 per block
- Modulation matrix with global and per-voice source values

## 5. src/audio/mixer/mixer-engine.ts
Lines 1-246. Mixer audio graph engine with per-track channel strips. Key features:
- Per-track chain: inputGain → insert chain → preFaderTap → faderGain → panner → muteGain → analyser → master
- Master bus: inputGain → faderGain → brickwall limiter → analyser → destination
- INV-2: Non-bypassable DynamicsCompressor limiter (threshold=-1dBFS, ratio=20:1)
- Mute/solo/solo-isolate logic
- Track input node access for synthesis routing
- Emergency mute/release capability
- Fader taper applied to level changes

## 6. src/state/undo/shared-undo-manager.ts
Lines 1-4. A minimal singleton export wrapping createUndoManager() function.

All files are complete and ready for review.

content.content:
Learned: When hardening audio engine code: (1) JavaScript main-thread code is single-threaded, so seek() updating two variables is already atomic from the audio thread's perspective since SAB writes are what the audio thread reads. (2) look-ahead-scheduler callbacks must be wrapped in try-catch or a single uncaught exception kills the setInterval. (3) AudioWorklet message handlers should validate message shape before casting.
